requirement: Buyer can initiate the supplier onboarding process by providing basic supplier information.
Supplier onboarding process undergoes internal approval workflow.
Supplier receives invitation to register with AECI and complete due diligence steps via email.
Supplier information is accurate and current.
Supplier certifications and contact information are visible.
Multiple channels of managing supplier information are eliminated.
Supplier is created in Coupa only after completing the onboarding process.
Supplier status can be managed, including purchase block, payment block, deactivation, and reactivation.
Approval workflows are in place for procurement, finance, general accounting, and master data teams.
Internal users can register new suppliers in Coupa for urgent or business-critical situations, adhering to governance and approval controls.
Users can update vendor information, including status changes, in Coupa.
The One-Time Vendor process enables faster vendor creation with a simplified approval flow and reduced data requirements, while ensuring basic compliance and procurement controls.
Coupa displays different status labels to help users quickly see the status of each supplier.
The supplier onboarding and management process will be standardized across geographies, with exceptions for Australia and other countries based on local guidelines and requirements.
Coupa supports auto-translation of standard information on the user interface for multiple languages, and custom information can be translated by geography-specific subject matter experts.
The system supports local language requirements.
